content.problemStatement:
Chef had three sequences of real numbers k1,k2,‚Ä¶,kNk_1, k_2, \ldots, k_Nk1‚Äč,k2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,kN‚Äč, c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äč,c2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äč and x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč. Unfortunately, he lost the sequence xxx; the only thing he remembers about it is that
x1‚čÖk1+x2‚čÖk2+‚Ä¶+xN‚čÖkN=0‚ÄČ.x_1 \cdot k_1 + x_2 \cdot k_2 + \ldots + x_N \cdot k_N = 0 \,.x1‚Äč‚čÖk1‚Äč+x2‚Äč‚čÖk2‚Äč+‚Ä¶+xN‚Äč‚čÖkN‚Äč=0.

Chef's favourite expression is
x1+c1+x2+c2+‚Ä¶+xN+cN‚ÄČ.\sqrt{x_1+c_1} + \sqrt{x_2+c_2} + \ldots + \sqrt{x_N+c_N} \,.x1‚Äč+c1‚Äč‚Äč+x2‚Äč+c2‚Äč‚Äč+‚Ä¶+xN‚Äč+cN‚Äč‚Äč.
Its value is defined only if xi+ci‚Č•0x_i+c_i \ge 0xi‚Äč+ci‚Äč‚Č•0 for each valid iii. Let's denote the maximum value of this expression  (over all sequences xxx such that it is defined) by FFF.

Help Chef find FFF and a sequence x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč such that the value of Chef's expression is FFF, or determine that there is no solution (the value of Chef's expression is always undefined).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
**Subtask #2 (15 points):** ki=1k_i = 1ki‚Äč=1 and ci=0c_i = 0ci‚Äč=0 for each valid iii
**Subtask #3 (15 points):** ki=1k_i = 1ki‚Äč=1 for each valid iii
**Subtask #4 (15 points):** ci=0c_i = 0ci‚Äč=0 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN real numbers k1,k2,‚Ä¶,kNk_1, k_2, \ldots, k_Nk1‚Äč,k2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,kN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN real numbers c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äč,c2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äč.
All real numbers are given with exactly two digits after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, if there is no solution, print a single line containing one integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1. Otherwise, print a single line containing N+1N+1N+1 space-separated real numbers F,x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNF, x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_NF,x1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč.

It can be proved that if a solution exists, then it is unique. Your answer will be considered correct if the absolute or relative error of each number on the output does not exceed 10‚ąí210^{‚ąí2}10‚ąí2.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The optimal solution is x=(28.6,2.4,‚ąí49/15,‚ąí5.9)x = (28.6, 2.4, -49/15, -5.9)x=(28.6,2.4,‚ąí49/15,‚ąí5.9). Then, x1‚čÖk1+x2‚čÖk2+x3‚čÖk3+x4‚čÖk4=28.6‚čÖ1+2.4‚čÖ2‚ąí49‚čÖ3/15+(‚ąí5.9)‚čÖ4=0x_1 \cdot k_1 + x_2 \cdot k_2 + x_3 \cdot k_3 + x_4 \cdot k_4 = 28.6 \cdot 1 + 2.4 \cdot 2 - 49 \cdot 3 / 15 + (-5.9) \cdot 4 = 0x1‚Äč‚čÖk1‚Äč+x2‚Äč‚čÖk2‚Äč+x3‚Äč‚čÖk3‚Äč+x4‚Äč‚čÖk4‚Äč=28.6‚čÖ1+2.4‚čÖ2‚ąí49‚čÖ3/15+(‚ąí5.9)‚čÖ4=0, and F=28.6+5+2.4+6+‚ąí49/15+7+‚ąí5.9+8‚Čź12.076F = \sqrt{28.6+5}+\sqrt{2.4+6}+\sqrt{-49/15+7}+\sqrt{-5.9+8} \doteq 12.076F=28.6+5‚Äč+2.4+6‚Äč+‚ąí49/15+7‚Äč+‚ąí5.9+8‚Äč‚Čź12.076. This is the maximum value of Chef's expression.
Example case 2: For each iii, cic_ici‚Äč is negative, but xi+cix_i+c_ixi‚Äč+ci‚Äč must be non-negative, so xix_ixi‚Äč must be positive. However, all kik_iki‚Äč are also positive, so x1k1+x2k2+‚Ä¶+xNkNx_1 k_1 + x_2 k_2 + \ldots + x_N k_Nx1‚Äčk1‚Äč+x2‚Äčk2‚Äč+‚Ä¶+xN‚ÄčkN‚Äč cannot be 000. There is no solution.
